prose: The sign uses a claw-like grasping hand in front of the chest, palm facing down, fingertips pointing forward. The hand closes and then opens once.

gaps.reason: 'claw-like' maps to either bent-5 (all five fingers bent) or bent-V (only index and middle bent); the two are phonologically distinct
gaps.suggested_question: Are all five fingers bent (bent-5), or only the index and middle (bent-V)?
